systemicBugs.bug: Connectivity loss / orphan nodes — non-bus nodes are not referenced by any edge source/target, so the diagram topology is missing. Includes SLDs with many nodes but 0-5 edges, indicating edges are dropped on save/import/sync rather than a user authoring choice.
systemicBugs.evidence: Extreme cases: 1b97ad10 'Test sync in ios offline' = 855/859 orphan with only 1 edge; 9138fd14 '(s) Wild Goose Brewery' = 488/489 orphan, 5 edges for 490 nodes; e8743699 'Android Site' = 1095 orphan, 122 edges for 1228 nodes; f676eb74 'Android QA SIte 1' = 212/225 orphan, 4 edges; 7a875534 'Test 12/4' = 214/217 orphan, 3 edges; 21bd10d4 'test site' = 1530/1540 orphan. Also 8 SLDs (4a2a7dbc, 5b90aac3, 1ac4aeb9, 5c47b38d, 88e2d3a0, 3029a5b8, eb3ab92e (292 nodes/0 edges), 3d37e08c) have nodes but literally 0 edges — full connectivity loss.

systemicBugs.bug: Default coordinate pile-up — nodes are persisted at the unplaced default positions (0,0) and (100,100) instead of real layout coordinates. Layout positions appear lost on create/import/sync; many SLDs have the majority of nodes stacked at one of two points.
systemicBugs.evidence: e8743699 'Android Site' = 754 nodes piled at (0,0); 701bcd9e 'Android sld site' = 127 nodes at default (100,100)/(0,0) (126 overlapping at (0,0)); 21bd10d4 'test site' = 187 at (100,100) + 102 at (0,0); 6e8e6fa5 'Quick Count on Web 2' = all 46 nodes unplaced (38 at (100,100), 8 at (0,0)); 24c97377 = all 29 at (100,100); 85c4c55a = all 10 at (100,100); c6b9efd2/3d37e08c = every node at (0,0).

systemicBugs.bug: Negative / out-of-range node coordinates — nodes persisted with x<0 or y<0, and in several cases wildly out-of-range values, indicating layout coordinate computation or import scaling is wrong (renders nodes off-canvas).
systemicBugs.evidence: e8743699 'Android Site' = 234 negative-coord nodes; 1b97ad10 = 648 negative; eb3ab92e 'Migration ios' = 222/292 negative; 21bd10d4 = 264 negative. Extreme out-of-range outliers: c2b9a0f8 'Android Test nauu' at (-16469.07,67771.54) and (-17347.63,-10264.26); f676eb74 at (16105.357,-12226.1875); 7627115e at (11336.6,-3480.6162); 768ff683 'Richmond, CA' at (49193.06,-3320.09); 6271fdc2 at (103105.0,-1888.39).

systemicBugs.bug: Unclassified edges — active edges returned with null/empty edge_class. Edge classification (which encodes electrical connection type) is not being populated/persisted; many SLDs are 100% unclassified.
systemicBugs.evidence: Whole-diagram unclassified: 5446bd9f 'QA-Site-1202' = 15/15 edges null; dff7591d 'Quick count copy test' = 5/5; 17078823 = 3/3; 825b59ac = 2/2; 0cef9db5 = 2/2; 848f0a69 = 3/3; 7e353179 'user A' = 5/5. High-volume: f3515650 'Test site' = 88/95 null; 160f9258 'Backword' = 22/24; 95aa5079 'Toronto, Canada' = 62/63; 701bcd9e = 82 active edges null; e8743699 = 75/122.

systemicBugs.bug: Duplicate node labels — many nodes share identical labels within one SLD (e.g. the same component name repeated up to 60x), breaking node identity/uniqueness and likely caused by copy/duplicate or import not de-duping or re-labeling.
systemicBugs.evidence: 21bd10d4 'test site' = 'Fuse 1' x60, 'UpdatedModel' x46, 'Fuse 2' x14 (26 dup labels); 6271fdc2 'Performance sld' = 162 duplicated labels incl. 'Spare' x13; e8743699 'Android Site' = 191 dup labels; 44db26b1 = 'App update check' x23; c041dfd1 = 'Test' x15; 768ff683 'Richmond, CA' = 'ATS 1' x9 (42 dup labels). Also 9138fd14 had a 258-char 'LongNameTest_AAA...' label x8 (label-length issue).

systemicBugs.bug: Soft-deleted edges leak through /api/sld detail/lookup — is_deleted=true edges are returned to the client instead of being filtered server-side, inflating edge counts and risking phantom connections in the UI.
systemicBugs.evidence: Confirmed not filtered server-side. 9138fd14 '(s) Wild Goose Brewery' = 253 deleted edges leaked (258 raw vs 5 active); 21bd10d4 'test site' = 181 leaked; b06b9bd9 'Yxbzjzxj' = 41; c2b9a0f8 'Android Test nauu' = 43; 768ff683 'Richmond, CA' = 34; fdc8898b 'London, UK' = 25 (only 9 of 34 active); 95aa5079 = 23; a70fcdc1 = 17 (only 6 of 23 active); 09fe7d5f, 9d171c47, c20f247b, e8743699, f8a1d7e7 all verified leaking deleted edges.

systemicBugs.bug: Node coordinate overlap — multiple distinct (non-deleted) nodes sit at identical coordinates, so they render exactly on top of each other. Largely a visible symptom of the default pile-up plus duplicated layout coords, but also occurs at non-default coords.

Isolated nodes with zero edges (total connectivity absence) — distinct from partial orphans: these SLDs persist nodes but no edges at all, including large diagrams, indicating edge data never saved or fully dropped on sync/import.
systemicBugs.evidence: eb3ab92e 'Migration ios' = 292 nodes / 0 edges (entire diagram disconnected); 1ac4aeb9 'Migration testing 31 to 32' = 19/0; 4d7e2443 'Bhhhh' = 76/0; 4e9f75ac 'test with nancy' = 10/0; 85c4c55a 'test' = 10/0; 6e8e6fa5 'Quick Count on Web 2' = 46/0; plus 4a2a7dbc, 88e2d3a0, 3029a5b8, 5c47b38d (latter retains 2 soft-deleted edges, 0 active). Strong migration/offline-sync correlation by SLD name.

result.summary: Scanned 107 SLDs; 87 had recorded findings (the other ~20 were clean). The findings collapse into 8 systemic, server/data-layer bugs that should gate the SLD v3 release. The two most pervasive are (1) connectivity loss — 82/107 SLDs have nodes that no edge references (orphans), including extreme cases where 100-1228-node diagrams carry only 1-5 edges, meaning the topology that drives single-line-diagram semantics is simply not persisted/returned; and (2) default coordinate pile-up — 79/107 SLDs leave nodes stacked at the unplaced defaults (0,0) and (100,100), e.g. Android Site stacks 754 nodes at (0,0). Both look like save/sync regressions where layout and edge data are dropped on create/import. Three data-correctness bugs follow: negative/out-of-range coordinates (61), unclassified edges with null/empty edge_class (54 — entire diagrams 100% unclassified), and soft-deleted edges leaking through /api/sld detail/lookup because is_deleted is not filtered server-side (35 SLDs, up to 253 leaked edges). Duplicate node labels (49) and node coordinate overlap (40, largely the same pile-up rendered visually) round it out. HIGH severity: connectivity loss, deleted-edge leak, unclassified edges, default pile-up. NOTE: the hypothesized "null/empty node name" bug was NOT observed in any SLD — all label findings are duplicates, not nulls; reporting accordingly.
